Operation 'scrapeGet' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'Basic Scrape' is too generic and doesn't indicate the HTTP method or action. Description lacks information about what is returned, available features, and how the API handles common scraping challenges. Enhanced version clarifies the GET method, explains key features available (JavaScript rendering, proxies, geolocation), specifies the response content, and explains the value proposition for AI agents deciding when to use this operation.

data.current_description: To scrape a web page using the scrapestack API, simply use the API's base endpoint and append the `URL` you would like to scrape as well as your API access key as GET parameters.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web page content via GET request
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes a web page by sending a GET request with the target URL and access key as parameters. Supports features including JavaScript rendering, custom HTTP headers, geolocation targeting, and residential proxy options. Returns the scraped HTML content along with metadata about the request. Handles IP blocks and CAPTCHAs transparently through the API infrastructure.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Basic Scrape' is too generic and doesn't indicate the HTTP method or action. Description lacks information about what is returned, available features, and how the API handles common scraping challenges. Enhanced version clarifies the GET method, explains key features available (JavaScript rendering, proxies, geolocation), specifies the response content, and explains the value proposition for AI agents deciding when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'scrapePost' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context, unclear_purpose. Original summary is unclear about the dual nature of the operation (scraping via POST). Description is too brief and doesn't explain the use case, what gets returned, or how it differs from GET. Enhanced version clarifies that this performs actual POST requests to targets (not just scraping), explains the request body forwarding capability, specifies the response content, and provides context about when an AI agent should choose this over GET requests.

data.current_summary: Basic Scrape — forward POST body to target
data.current_description: Send a POST request to `/scrape` to perform a POST (or form) request to the target URL.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web page with POST request forwarding
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes a web page by forwarding a POST request to the target URL. Allows sending form data, JSON payloads, or custom request bodies to the target while leveraging scrapestack's infrastructure for handling IP blocks, CAPTCHAs, JavaScript rendering, and geolocation targeting. Returns the scraped HTML response from the target server along with request metadata. Useful for scraping pages that require form submissions or API interactions.
data.reasoning: Original summary is unclear about the dual nature of the operation (scraping via POST). Description is too brief and doesn't explain the use case, what gets returned, or how it differs from GET. Enhanced version clarifies that this performs actual POST requests to targets (not just scraping), explains the request body forwarding capability, specifies the response content, and provides context about when an AI agent should choose this over GET requests.

message: Operation 'scrapePut' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context, unclear_purpose. Original summary is unclear about the dual nature of the operation (scraping via PUT). Description relies on comparison to POST rather than standing alone, lacks output details, and doesn't explain the use case. Enhanced version provides independent clarity about PUT method usage, specifies what gets returned, explains the value of request body and header forwarding, and helps AI agents understand when to use PUT versus POST or GET methods for scraping.

data.current_summary: Basic Scrape — forward PUT body to target
data.current_description: Same behaviour as POST but using the HTTP PUT method. The API will forward your PUT body and headers to the target.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web page with PUT request forwarding
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes a web page by forwarding a PUT request to the target URL. Allows sending request bodies and custom headers to the target while leveraging scrapestack's infrastructure for handling IP blocks, CAPTCHAs, JavaScript rendering, and geolocation targeting. Returns the scraped HTML response from the target server along with request metadata. Useful for scraping pages that require PUT method requests or RESTful API interactions.
data.reasoning: Original summary is unclear about the dual nature of the operation (scraping via PUT). Description relies on comparison to POST rather than standing alone, lacks output details, and doesn't explain the use case. Enhanced version provides independent clarity about PUT method usage, specifies what gets returned, explains the value of request body and header forwarding, and helps AI agents understand when to use PUT versus POST or GET methods for scraping.
